failure_surface: The execution code contained syntax errors preventing successful script execution, and when code did run, the solution only modified row 16 (Electricity) instead of the complete required range C15:G16 on the INPUTS sheet.
root_cause: Two failures occurred: (1) Malformed function call syntax caused repeated SyntaxErrors during code submission, preventing valid execution; (2) The agent misread the answer position requirement by applying formulas only to row 16 while the specification indicated C15:G16, requiring updates to both rows even though row 15 already appeared functional.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure all code submissions use properly closed XML-like function tags, and always verify the complete target cell range from the task specifications before limiting formula application to a subset of rows.
evidence: Multiple SyntaxError messages showing unterminated string literals on line 3 indicate malformed function tags in tool calls
Answer position specified as 'INPUTS'!C15:G16 but code only wrote formulas to row 16 (for col in range(3, 8) targets row=16 only)
Test case results show eval value of [0], indicating the output file did not pass verification
User stated C15:G15 displayed correctly already, but the task required the solution to work for the full C15:G16 range
The solution code set cell.value = '' then assigned formula, but never actually ran due to earlier syntax errors

memory_items.title: Validate Tool Call Syntax Before Code Execution
memory_items.lesson: Malformed function tags will prevent any code from executing, wasting valuable attempts.
memory_items.generalization: Always verify that function call structures are properly formed with matching opening and closing tags before submitting execution requests. Check that special characters within strings do not break XML parsing.

memory_items.title: Honor Complete Target Range Specifications
memory_items.lesson: Even if some cells appear correct, the specified answer range must be fully covered by the solution.
memory_items.generalization: Extract the exact cell range from the task specification and ensure all cells within that range receive appropriate updates, regardless of whether certain rows initially display correct values. Verify row counts match expected deltas after writing.

memory_items.title: Formula vs. Static Value Awareness in openpyxl
memory_items.lesson: When using openpyxl, assigning formula requires setting the formula attribute separately from the value.
memory_items.generalization: For formula cells, clear any existing value with cell.value = None or '', then assign the formula string to cell.formula. Do not mix static calculations into formula strings unless the final output should be a value rather than a dynamic reference.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Function Call Integrity
- Always validate XML-like function tags are properly closed before submitting code.
- Syntax errors in tool calls prevent any downstream logic from executing.

### Complete Range Coverage
- Extract and honor the full target cell range from specifications.
- Even if some cells within the range appear correct, apply consistent formula coverage across the entire specified region.
- Validate row count matches expected delta after writing.

### Formula Assignment via openpyxl
- For cells requiring formulas: clear existing values (cell.value = None), then assign to cell.formula attribute.
- Ensure formula strings use standard Excel notation without Python-style concatenation errors.
